name: 1. Exact path
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob

name: 2. Multiple exact paths
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/status
identity:
  name: bob

name: 3. Greedy glob
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
  name: bob

name: 4. Glob + capture + contains
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /data/x/b/info
identity:
  name: bob

name: 5. Method
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 6. Methods
input:
request:
  method: HEAD
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 7. Capture checked against trait
rule:
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    methods: [GET]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

name: 8. Capture equals identity
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/user/alice
identity:
  name: alice

name: 9. Role required
input:
request:
  method: DELETE
  path: /admin/settings
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [admin]

name: 10. Carve-out (negation)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 11. Literal chain, nested form
rule:
app_resources:
  # literal() splits its text on "/", so this nested chain is the same
  # matcher as the contracted literal("api/v4/buildinfo").
  - where: |-
      path.match(
        literal(
          "api",
          literal(
            "v4",
            literal(
              "buildinfo"))))
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/buildinfo
identity:
  name: bob

name: 12. Additive list (two rules)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /static/css/app.css
identity:
  name: bob

name: 13. Denied: wrong method
input:
request:
  method: POST
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob

name: 14. Anti-pattern (commented out)
rule:
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - "/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"
      # - "/api/status"   # Invalid: {project} must be bound for every path
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

name: 15. URL decoding: strict default rejects %2F
rule:
app_resources:
  # Default url_decoding: no decode, and any percent byte is rejected.
  - paths: ["/files/**"]
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob

name: 16. URL decoding: one pass admits %2F
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob

name: 17. URL decoding: one pass rejects %252F
rule:
app_resources:
  # One pass leaves a residual % in a double-encoded path, so it is rejected.
  - paths: ["/files/*/*"]
    url_decoding:
      decode_iterations: 1
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%252Fb
identity:
  name: bob

name: 18. URL decoding: two passes admit %252F
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%252Fb
identity:
  name: bob

name: 19. URL decoding: allow_percent keeps literal %
rule:
app_resources:
  # allow_percent admits a residual %, so a%2Fb stays one segment.
  - paths: ["/files/*"]
    url_decoding:
      allow_percent: true
input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob
